mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum and consumer income expansion, which drives aggregate demand and equity valuations upward over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2013 payroll growth at 135,195 thousand persons
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000 persons.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero (below 0.25%) while inflation measured by CPIAUCSL stays below 2.5%, signaling monetary accommodation that reduces equity risk premiums and supports forward equity returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with subdued inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2% signals weakening consumer demand and forward-looking earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices adjust to reduced growth expectations.
regime_hint: any regime with demand-sensitive equity valuations
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of occurrences, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 3-month moving average, signaling strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and corporate profitability expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: economic expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of months where INDPRO is above its 3-month moving average, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rise above 900,000 units annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously increase, indicating synchronized demand recovery across residential and consumer sectors that supports equity valuations over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 900,000 units AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:February 2013 HOUST at 917,000 units, March 2013 RSAFS at 418,284 million
regime_hint: post-financial-crisis housing recovery with synchronized demand expansion
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration and forward earnings risk that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation trend) turn negative and widen, indicating monetary policy accommodation that erodes bond yields and redirects capital flows to equities, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing with negative real rates
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 42% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) simultaneously rises, signaling synchronized supply-side weakness and labor market stress that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.5% AND UNRATE month-over-month change > +0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:February 2013 INDPRO at 99.0446, March 2013 UNRATE at 7.6%
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.
